steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: 'the guide soon grew in scope and influence' and 'its modest French eateries were replaced with some of the most esteemed restaurants from around the world, including Ishikawa in Tokyo and Jade Dragon in Macau'.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: The rule is: a comma + coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses. Choice C—'influence, and'—provides exactly this structure. A and B lack the coordinating conjunction, creating comma splices; D has 'and' but lacks the comma before it. That matches choice C — C is correct.
